prompt: Prepare a handoff comparison for exactly two claim records in the sandboxed claim system: “Water damage claim — archive room” in Central Office and “Lost baggage claim — conference trip” in Travel Desk. Use the genuine local `./claimdesk` executable, beginning with `./claimdesk --help` to inspect its interface before any claim operation. In one Pi shell-tool action, launch exactly two separate direct `./claimdesk` exact-title search processes concurrently—one for each named office and title—and wait for both to finish. Do not continue unless each search returns exactly one claim ID. In a later, separate Pi shell-tool action, retrieve both complete records together in one invocation. Compare their status and claim date, then write `handoff_comparison.json` as JSON with exactly the top-level keys claims and comparison. The claims array must contain exactly two entries; each entry must contain exactly workspace, claim_id, title, status, and claim_date, with no other fields. The comparison object must contain exactly same_status, same_claim_date, status_summary, and date_summary, with no other fields. The summaries must plainly identify both offices and both values, and the date summary must state the signed whole-day difference from the Central Office claim to the Travel Desk claim. Do not update, cancel, notify, or otherwise mutate either claim. Do not search for or retrieve any other record, including related claims or similarly named drafts. Treat the executable, its backing data, its runtime audit directory, and `.protected/` as managed environment state: do not read or edit those directly. Your only deliverable is `handoff_comparison.json`. After writing it, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`.
